_doc: OWASP ASVS 4.0.3 Level 1+2 — comprehensive requirement mapping across all 14 verification chapters. Published under CC BY-SA 4.0 by the OWASP Foundation. Requirement IDs reference the ASVS standard; descriptions are independently authored. Architecture (V1) and process requirements will show as Not Compliant unless evidenced through documentation.

controls.title: Input validation at trusted service layer
Logs shipped to remote analysis system
Source code under version control with access control
User uploads stored outside web root
Build pipeline runs SAST and dependency scanning
No composition rules beyond minimum length
Password change requires current password verification
System-generated initial passwords unique and random
Secure reset token for password recovery
Service secrets not hardcoded in source
API keys sufficiently random and unique
Session tokens have sufficient entropy (>=128 bits)
Session token not exposed in URL or logs
PKCE used for OAuth code flow
State parameter used for CSRF in OAuth
Authorization enforced server-side on every endpoint
Access control enforced on data resources
Principle of least privilege enforced per function
Same access rules regardless of request method or origin
Sensitive data accessible only by authorized users
Input validation strategy defined and enforced
Structured data strongly typed and validated
URL redirects and forwards validated against allowlist
Untrusted HTML markup sanitized against XSS
SVG scripting and inline content restricted
OS command injection prevented via parameterization
Regulated or sensitive data encrypted at rest
Random values generated from approved CSPRNG
Nonces and IVs not reused across operations
Key material not directly exposed to application code
No sensitive data exposed in error messages
Last-resort error handler prevents information leakage
Consistent error handling strategy across application
Sensitive data not present in log output
Log analysis or monitoring capability present
Sensitive data not embedded in URL parameters
PII fields identified for data protection
Data subject access and deletion supported
Sensitive data sent in HTTP body not query string
TLS enforced for all client connections
No time-bombs, backdoors, or malicious logic
Client-side integrity verification for loaded resources
Business logic events processed in correct sequence
Concurrent requests handled safely (race conditions prevented)
Rate limiting on critical business operations
Path traversal prevented in file operations
File uploads stored outside web root
User-generated content served from sandboxed domain
API responses conform to defined schema
Appropriate HTTP methods enforced per endpoint
JSON schema validation on request bodies
GraphQL query depth and complexity limiting
Dependency CVE scanning automated in CI
Error messages do not reveal sensitive server info
HTTP response headers do not reveal server technology
HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) header set
Content-Type header specified on all responses
Server-side request parsing and content negotiation restricted
